info.name: Piranha — headless API
info.description:
Run the whole Piranha orchestrator from the command line, no UI. The browser app is only a client of this API; every endpoint below is what it calls. Start the backend with `pnpm run db:server:stable` + `pnpm run db:orchestrator:stable` (or `pnpm run agents` and just ignore the browser).

Set the `baseUrl` and `project` collection variables, then walk the folders top to bottom: create a task, trigger it, watch it move through the workflow, review its diff, approve it.

request.description: 200 { ok: true } when the db-server is up.
Live snapshot: orchestrator activity, active agents, circuit state, and the recent event feed for this project.
Every project. `default` maps to whatever repo you cloned into it.
A project only dispatches tasks once it is ready: a cloned git repo, a confirmed run-config, and a verified preview.

item.name: List tasks (poll this to watch progress)

request.description: Every task in the project. Watch `status`, `stage`, `lastOutcome`, `qaVerdict` change as agents run.

body.raw:
{
  "id": "{{taskId}}",
  "title": "Add a slugify(text) utility with a unit test",
  "description": "Add a pure function slugify(text) to src/utils that lowercases, trims, replaces runs of non-alphanumeric characters with a single hyphen, and strips leading/trailing hyphens. Export it from src/utils/index.ts. Add a unit test. Touch no other file.",
  "status": "TODO",
  "priority": 0
}

request.description: Create a task. `description` becomes the immutable `intent` the owner writes scenarios against. It starts in TODO — trigger it next to launch.
Moves the task to WORKING and wakes the orchestrator. It then walks the workflow (intake → plan → build → qa → accept → review) on its own.

item.name: Changes (the diff — review a non-visual task)

request.description: The diff of the task's branch vs the base: changed files with +/- counts, the commits, the full unified diff, and the QA verdict. This is how you verify a backend/library task that has nothing to preview.
Approve a task parked at the human-review gate. Reports the gate's `approved` outcome; the graph routes it onward (to merge in the default pipeline).
Reject at the review gate. Reports the gate's `rejected` outcome; the graph routes it back (to build in the default pipeline).

Update task fields. This is also the verb AGENTS use — `{"outcome":"pass"}` to advance, `{"reject":"why"}` to bounce, `{"consult":{"to":"plan","question":"..."}}` to ask a peer. Control-plane fields (handoffFrom, hops) are stripped from any caller.
The project's workflow document (or the built-in default when none is saved), plus whether it validates and which stages are occupied by live tasks.

Replace the workflow. `expectedRev` must match the current rev or the save is rejected (409) so two editors can't clobber each other. An invalid graph returns 422 naming the problems; a graph that would strand a live task returns 409. GET it first, edit the `doc`, PUT it back with its `rev` as `expectedRev`.

item.name: Reset workflow to the built-in pipeline

body.raw:
{
  "query": "clamp a value between min and max",
  "projectId": "{{project}}",
  "topK": 8,
  "agentName": "cli",
  "taskId": "{{taskId}}"
}

request.description: Semantic search over the project's code index. Also feeds the shared context: the matching files are recorded so the next agent is told to read them first. Returns { results, remembered, evicted }.
The agent roster (owner, architect, dev, qa, + any custom) with their models, prompt templates and worktree modes.
The git worktree each task built in, its branch, last commit, and whether it merged.
